what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 inline SVG outline icon showing three circular nodes connected by curved path strokes, with a small directional chevron near the right node.
observations: Uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with explicit width and height of 24.
Draws three circles at top-left, bottom-center, and top-right positions.
Uses two cubic curve segments to connect the side nodes toward the lower center node.
Includes a final polyline-like path forming a small angled marker near the right circle.
Uses no fill and currentColor stroke for inherited coloring.
standouts: Three-node layout with a lower central node creates a branching flow shape.
Curved connector paths imply movement or routing between points.
The right-side angled marker adds directionality.
conventions: Follows compact SVG icon markup with inline geometry.
Uses outline-icon conventions: fill none, currentColor stroke, rounded caps, rounded joins, and 1.5 stroke width.
Keeps all drawing primitives inside a single svg element without groups or metadata.
concerns: No title or desc element is present for accessible labeling.
Single-line minified markup is less readable for manual editing.
notes: This file is a small reusable outline icon for a path or flow concept.
questions: Is the right-side angled marker intended to be an arrowhead, a chevron, or a separate route indicator?
Should this icon include accessible metadata, or is it always decorative?
Is the 1.5 stroke width part of the broader icon set contract?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a path-flow icon, and the SVG content matches that by depicting connected nodes, curved routes, and directional movement.
intention_signals: It appears intended to communicate movement through connected points or branching paths in a compact design-system icon.
novelty: The icon combines a three-node flow diagram with a small directional marker integrated near the right node.
contribution: Contributes a currentColor, stroke-based path-flow glyph that can be retained as a reusable visual substrate for navigation, routing, branching, or process-flow concepts.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG outline icon depicting three circular nodes connected by curved flow paths with a small directional marker.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing path flow, routing, or connected movement as a reusable design-system icon.
